title: modes::Modifier::Debug and Preset::Debug share name with serde rename_all collision risk
description:
## Severity
Trivial
## Dimension
naming
## Location
src/modes/mod.rs:67-104 (Modifier and Preset enums)
## Violation
Both `Modifier` and `Preset` have a variant named `Debug`:

```rust
pub enum Modifier { Bold, Debug, Methodical, Director, Readonly, ContextPacing }
pub enum Preset   { Create, Extend, Safe, Refactor, Explore, Debug, Methodical, Director }
```

Three variant names overlap (`Debug`, `Methodical`, `Director`) across the two enums. When both are serialized in the same JSON document via serde (e.g., a session-state dump or config slice), readers cannot disambiguate without the enclosing field name. The `Display` impl produces the same string ("debug") for both, so log lines like `"mode: debug"` are ambiguous.

This is a naming-discipline violation per §3.3: enum variants should be uniquely identifiable in their domain. The Preset::Debug variant is also confusingly close to the standard library `std::fmt::Debug` trait — the literal name "Debug" inside `match preset { Preset::Debug => ... }` reads as a trait reference rather than a variant.
## Mandated refactor
Rename `Preset::Debug` → `Preset::Investigate` (matches the existing "Investigation-first debugging" doc-comment). Rename `Preset::Methodical` and `Modifier::Methodical` to disambiguate by prefix (`Preset::MethodicalRollout` vs `Modifier::Methodical`) OR document the intentional overlap and add a `#[serde(rename = "preset-debug")]` discriminator on Preset variants so JSON consumers can't confuse them.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
